channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Northern group defends Tinubu over NNPCL row
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A Northern group defends President Tinubu amidst the NNPCL row, dismissing ethnic bias allegations in recruitment and operations. Read more.
Read More: https://punchng.com/northern-group-defends-tinubu-over-nnpcl-row/

item.title: One dies as Niger battles infectious disease outbreak
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
One child has died in a suspected infectious disease outbreak in Niger State, prompting a comprehensive investigation and NCDC intervention. Learn more abo
Read More: https://punchng.com/one-dies-as-niger-battles-infectious-disease-outbreak/

item.title: Overlooked power of social influence in climate education
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The greatest missing ingredient in climate education is not information but social influence. The post Overlooked power of social influence in climate education appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Court sentences man to death for killing daughter
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Justice Blessing Ewu of High Court No. 11, Calabar, has sentenced Apostle Ubong Bassey Etim to death by hanging for the murder of his 16-year-old daughter, Deborah Bassey. The court heard that Etim falsely accused his daughter, who had Down syndrome, of witchcraft before killing her. Delivering the

item.title: FCCPC cleared to enforce digital lending regulations
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Commission (FCCPC) has announced the resumption of the implementation and enforcement of the Digital, Electronic, Online or Non-Traditional Consumer Lending Regulations, 2025 (DEON Regulations), following the Federal High Court judgment affirming its a

item.title: Guyana ferry capsizes, leaving at least 27 dead, 83 missing
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: At least 27 people died after a ferry capsized off Guyana, Prime Minister Mark Phillips said Monday, as rescuers searched for 83 people still missing. Sixty-nine passengers have been rescued since the MV Barima overturned Saturday night while traveling from Georgetown to the inland Essequibo region,

item.title: Navy busts illegal refineries in Rivers
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Nigerian Navy has dismantled two reactivated illegal refining sites in Rivers State and recovered more than 126,000 litres of suspected stolen crude oil and illegally refined Automotive Gas Oil (AGO) in a renewed crackdown on crude oil theft. The operation, conducted under Operation DELTA SENTIN

item.title: Protests rock Osun motor parks as NURTW faction rejects chairman, alleges killings
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Members of the National Union of Road Transport Workers (NURTW) on Monday staged protests across major motor parks in Osun State, passing a vote of no confidence in the leadership of the factional state chairman, Nurudeen Alowonle. The post Protests rock Osun motor parks as NURTW faction rejects cha

item.title: Police arrest 5 over fake bank alerts, recover suspected counterfeit foreign currencies
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Yobe State Police Command has arrested five suspects over alleged fraud involving fake bank transfer alerts and recovered suspected counterfeit foreign currencies during a raid on their hideout. The command’s spokesman, SP Dungus Abdulkarim, disclosed this in a statement issued in Damaturu. He s

item.title: Delta police foil cult initiation, recover firearms, vehicles
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Delta State Police Command has foiled a suspected cult initiation ceremony and recovered firearms, ammunition, seven vehicles and other exhibits during an intelligence-led operation in Orogun Community, Ughelli North Local Government. The post Delta police foil cult initiation, recover firearms

item.title: Victims tell court how kidnappers collected over N63m in ransom
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Victims of the Vegetable Market kidnapping incident and other abduction cases in Edo State have recounted in court how kidnappers collected more than N63 million in ransom before releasing them. The victims, who testified as prosecution witnesses in the trial of an alleged kidnapping syndicate befor

item.title: Court grants ex-Warri Refinery MD N500m bail in N1.339bn fraud case
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) on Monday arraigned the former Managing Director of the Warri Refining and Petrochemical Company Limited, Jimoh Olasunkanmi Yisawu, before the Federal High Court in Abuja over an alleged N1.339 billion money laundering scheme. The post Court grants

item.title: The Story Behind Nicolas Williams Giving His FIFA World Cup Winner’s Medal to His Mother
item.dc:creator: BellaNaija
item.description: After helping Spain lift the 2026 World Cup, Nico Williams placed his gold medal around his mother María's neck. Here is the emotional story of sacrifice and Ghanaian roots behind that viral moment. The post The Story Behind Nicolas Williams Giving His FIFA World Cup Winner’s Medal to His Moth

item.title: 4 suspects arrested over lynching of 21-year-old
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Akwa Ibom State Police Command has arrested four suspects allegedly involved in the brutal killing of a 21-year-old man. It was gathered that Emmanuel Effiong was lynched by a mob after being accused of stealing chickens and eggs from a poultry farm in Mbak 4 community, Uyo Local Government Area

item.title: Nigeria saves N1trn through budget evaluation – BPP
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Bureau of Public Procurement (BPP) has reported a total saving of N1.1 trillion for the Federal government, attributing it to efficient budget evaluation. Director-General of the Bureau of Public Procurement (BPP), Dr. Adebowale A. Adedokun stated this when the Nigerian Institute of Quantity Sur

item.title: Islamic finance key to ending Northern Nigeria’s financial exclusion – OIC
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Secretary-General of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation Arbitration Centre (OIC-AC), Dr Umar A. Oseni, has said expanding Islamic finance is critical to reducing financial exclusion and unlocking economic growth in Northern Nigeria. Speaking as the keynote speaker at the Arewa Islamic Finan

item.title: Africa must lead digital policy standards – NCC
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Executive Vice-Chairman (EVC) of the Nigerian Communications Commission (NCC), Aminu Maida says Africa should play a bigger role in shaping global standards on artificial intelligence (AI), spectrum management, cybersecurity and digital governance rather than merely adopting them. Maida spoke on

item.title: 2026 NBA Elections: A Credibility Crisis?

item.description: The Nigerian Bar Association’s general elections held on Saturday, July 18, 2026, have been widely condemned by leading candidates as deeply flawed and lacking credibility. Despite 82,164 accredited voters, turnout

item.title: Ekiti grows monthly IGR to N2.7b

item.description: The Executive Chairman, Ekiti State Internal Revenue Service, (EKIRS), Mr. Olaniyan Olatona, has disclosed that the monthly Internally Generated Revenue, IGR, in the state has increased to N2.7 billion. item.description: The Court of Appeal, Lagos Division, has affirmed the judgement of the Federal High Court awarding N5 million in damages against the Inspector-General of Police (IGP) and other Police officers

item.title: 300 Benue smallholder farmers receive improved vegetable seeds
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: At least 300 smallholder farmers in Benue State have received improved vegetable seeds in a move aimed at increasing household income and improving food security through year-round vegetable production. item.title: Why are gasoline prices rising faster than oil prices? Blame it on the ‘crack.’
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The spread between the cost of barrels of gasoline and crude oil — known as the “crack spread” — has widened sharply.

item.title: Menzies shocks world No 2 Humphries in World Matchplay thriller
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Cameron Menzies produced a massive upset to defeat 2024 champion Luke Humphries 10-7 at the World Matchplay on Monday night.

item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: New report denounces Havana and claims ‘campaign of subversion’ has infiltrated highest levels of US governmentAs part of the Trump administration’s continuing attack on left-leaning groups, the US state department has listed several activists and organizations as “front groups and fellow travelers”

item.title: New Zealand’s Faster Inflation Bolsters Case for More Rate Hikes
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: New Zealand’s annual inflation accelerated to the fastest pace in more than two years, fanned by soaring fuel prices and bolstering the case for the Reserve Bank to execute back-to-back interest-rate increases.

item.title: Kioxia Bulls Ride Out Volatility With Targets 130% Above Market
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Analysts are maintaining bullish views on Kioxia Holdings Inc. despite a deepening share price correction, saying the stock is likely to resume its upward trajectory once supply-demand conditions improve.

item.title: LA’s $1.2 Billion ‘Graffiti Towers’ Cleared to Exit Bankruptcy
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The bankrupt $1.2 billion Oceanwide Plaza project in Los Angeles, dubbed the Graffiti Towers after taggers spray-painted the abandoned complex, has been cleared for sale and possible completion more than seven years after the original Chinese developer ran out of money.

item.title: New UK PM Burnham stamps authority with cabinet shakeup
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The monarch's fourth prime minister since he acceded to the throne in 2022, and Britain's seventh in just over a decade, Burnham said the country needed to show the world it could "regain stability". The post New UK PM Burnham stamps authority with cabinet shakeup appeared first on Vanguard News.

Speaking during the event on Monday, Governor Sule said the construction of the road was a deliberate effort of his administration to link rural communit

item.description: Sony Music Entertainment has filed another lawsuit against Udio, accusing the AI music generator of infringing the copyright of more than 30,000 of its songs, ranging from Elvis Presley's Hound Dog to Beyoncé's Say My Name, and Harry Styles' As It Was. The lawsuit, filed in a New York court on

Bello Isah Ambarura, a member representing Illela/Gwadabawa Federal Constituency in the House of Representatives, as the Deputy Governorship candidate to Hon. Manir Muhammad Dan-Iya, the party’s Governo

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The federal government said it has revived 16 abandoned digital e-library projects across Federal Unity Colleges and commissioned a state-of-the-art digital hub at the federal government College, Kano, as part of efforts to modernise public schools and expand access to technology-driven learning. Th

item.title: The FCC is planning to retroactively ban disguised DJI gadgets

item.description: Last October, we told you how the FCC had given itself the power to retroactively ban gadgets that have already received its approval to be imported and sold in the United States. Now, the FCC's getting ready to wield that power for the first time, by cracking down on the "DJI front companies" suspe

item.description: The Trump administration on Monday said it would slap 50% tariffs on some goods imported from Canada, in an effort to end what it sees as discriminatory trade practices by Ottawa against the U.S. automobile, dairy and alcohol industries.

item.title: The Odyssey turned me into an IMAX believer
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: After seeing Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey for the first time early last week, I came away impressed, but somewhat conflicted about two of the film's more fantastical set pieces. While those scenes were beautifully crafted and tremendously acted, there was something a little bit off about the way

item.title: Chevron Spends Over $10bn On Local Content In Nigeria
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Chevron Nigeria Limited on Monday disclosed that it has spent over $ 10 billion on Local content in Nigeria in the past 10 years. The Manager, Communications, Chevron, Dr Victor Anyaegbudike, explained that the company takes local content development seriously and is committed to ensuring the develo

item.title: Okpebholo Suspends Commissioner, LPRES Coordinator Over Alleged Official Infractions
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Edo State governor, Senator Monday Okpebholo, has approved the immediate suspension of the Commissioner for Livestock Development, Prof. Omorodion Ignatius Ikponmwosa, and the State Project Coordinator of the Livestock Productivity and Resilience Support (LPRES) Project, Mrs. Ikpikhumi Betsy Aghaku,

item.title: Kaduna ADC Leaders End Factional Rift After Reconciliation Meeting
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Factional leaders of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) in Kaduna State have resolved their differences following a reconciliation meeting convened by the party’s 2023 governorship candidate, Hon. Isa Ashiru.   The meeting, which lasted about three hours at Ashiru’s office

item.title: Trump signs order imposing new 50% tariffs on certain Canadian goods
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US President Donald Trump signed orders on Monday imposing new 50% tariffs on some Canadian goods, citing what the White House called Canada's "discriminatory treatment" of US alcohol, automobiles and dairy products. The tariffs take effect in 30 days, with energy, potash and sector-specific imports

item.title: 2026 World Cup: FIFA opens investigation into Spain vs Argentina final
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: FIFA has opened a disciplinary investigation into the aftermath of the 2026 World Cup final between Argentina and Spain. There were ugly scenes at the end of the game, involving players and coaches from both teams. The game was ultimately decided by Ferran Torres’ goal in the 106th minute. Bef

item.title: Police arrest suspected motorcycle thief, recover 7 bikes
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Ekiti State Police Command has recorded another major breakthrough in its fight against crime with the arrest of one suspected motorcycle thief, two suspected receivers of stolen property, and the recovery of seven motorcycles believed to have been stolen. The arrest was made on Saturday, July 1

item.title: ‘The Pain Is Immense,’ Says Messi After World Cup Final Defeat
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: At the World Cup, he has now lost two finals, in 2014 and 2026, and won one, in 2022, against France. The post ‘The Pain Is Immense,’ Says Messi After World Cup Final Defeat appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Lebanese army deploys to three southern pilot zones under US-backed security framework
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The Lebanese army began assuming security responsibilities in three southern villages on Monday under a US-backed pilot initiative, the State Department said. Operations in Froun, Srifa and Zawtar al-Gharbiya mark the first implementation of a framework aimed at enabling a phased Israeli withdrawal
